findings.summary: Complex RP2350 SBC with DVI, USB hub, audio amplifier - rich signal analysis with 8 voltage dividers, 17 RC filters, power regulators, and audio signal paths all detected; PWM audio filter topology and ferrite bead classification are notable strengths; crystal circuits still undetected

correct.description: TPS62A02 switching regulator correctly identified with feedback divider R25/R26 (220k/49.9k), ratio 0.185, estimated Vout 3.25V
MT3608 boost converter correctly identified with feedback divider R23/R24 (8.25k/1.05k), ratio 0.113, estimated Vout 5.31V
CH217K USB power switch correctly identified as LDO for +5V_USB_HOST
PWM audio low-pass filter R48/R53 (270R/150R to AUD-L) correctly detected as voltage divider with 0.357 ratio - this is the PWM-to-analog filter
Matched stereo audio filters (AUD-L and AUD-R channels) both detected with identical component values
Battery sensing divider R36/R37 (470k/470k) with 0.5 ratio correctly identified on GPIO47/BAT_SENS
Power sensing divider R34/R35 (2.2k/4.7k) with 0.681 ratio on GPIO46/PWR_SENS correctly found
VBUSM sensing divider R29/R30 (4.7k/10k) for USB hub correctly detected
Audio amplifier supply filter R99 100R with parallel caps (1.1uF, fc=1.45kHz) correctly identified
Analog audio domain RC filter R73 10R with 3 parallel caps (11.1uF, fc=1.43kHz) for +3.3VA correctly detected
Multiple audio RC coupling networks (R68/C63, R82/C78, R81/C76, R69/C65 at fc=15.9Hz) correctly identified as AC coupling
Audio MCLK RC filter R50 33R / C55 27pF at 178.6MHz correctly detected
I2S DAC RC filters (R46/R47 33R with C49/C50 20pF at 241MHz) correctly found
Audio codec crystal RC filters (R91/R104 1k with C90/C93/C81/C91 270pF at 589kHz) correctly detected
Volume control RC filter R80 220k / C79 1uF at 0.72Hz correctly identified for slow volume ramping
LC filter L3 4.7uH / 44uF at DCDC_VIN with 11.07kHz resonance correctly detected
Ferrite beads correctly classified as ferrite_bead type (4 FB components)
16 power rails including separate analog domains (+3.3VA, +5VA, GNDA, PWR_CC1/CC2, VREG_AVDD) correctly enumerated
Decoupling analysis shows comprehensive coverage across +3.3V (105.6uF/22 caps), +1V1, +5V, +5V_USB, +5V_USB_HOST rails

incorrect.description: Audio codec crystal circuit components (R91/R104 with C90/C93/C81/C91) classified as generic RC low-pass filters - these are actually crystal load network components for an audio codec crystal oscillator
Crystal feedback resistor R13 1k with 27pF caps classified as RC filter at 5.89MHz - this is crystal oscillator biasing, not an RC filter

AMP volume control network (R77 10k / R42 1k) classified as voltage divider but this is actually part of the digital volume control circuit for the audio amplifier where T2 is used as a variable attenuator

missed.description: No crystal circuits detected - RP2350 uses a 12MHz crystal with 27pF load caps (C21/C22) visible in BOM
DVI connector (DVI1) present with 8 series resistors (R59-R66) for TMDS differential pairs but no DVI/HDMI interface detection
USB hub IC not detected (likely FE1.1s or similar given USB_HOST1-2 and USB_HOST3-4 connectors)
Audio amplifier IC not detected as subcircuit despite extensive audio signal path analysis
Power on/off switch circuit (PWR_ON/OFF1) with PWR_SW rail not analyzed as power management subcircuit
Line-In audio connector and headset connector not linked to audio signal path

suggestions: PWM audio filter detection is a strength - the divider + multi-cap topology for PWM-to-analog is correctly found, though it could be explicitly labeled as PWM DAC filter
Crystal circuit detector needs to handle both standard crystal (high-value feedback R + matched load caps) and codec crystal (lower value R with matched caps) patterns
DVI/HDMI interface detection should look for clusters of matched series resistors (typically 33-100R) connecting to DVI/HDMI connector footprints
The separate analog ground domain (GNDA) and analog supply filtering are well-detected strengths of the analyzer

findings.summary: RP2350pc PCB: copper_layers_used includes F.Mask (non-copper layer), connector shield pads misidentified as thermal pads, zone stitching via areas inflated by per-polygon calculation

correct.description: Footprint count and board dimensions consistent with RP2350 SBC
SMD/THT breakdown reasonable for modern mixed-technology board
DFM analysis present with appropriate tier
Power net routing analysis covers supply rails

incorrect.description: copper_layers_used includes F.Mask which is a solder mask layer, not copper. Board should be 2 copper layers (F.Cu, B.Cu).
Connector shield/structural pads (USB, HDMI, SD card connectors) with no net are misidentified as thermal pads
Zone stitching via density uses per-polygon areas instead of per-net totals, inflating density numbers

suggestions: Filter thermal pad detection to exclude pads on connectors with no net
Use per-net total zone area for stitching via density calculation
